reasoning: The market estimated 11% on Oct 30, 2025. This tells us the event had not yet occurred between April 5 and Oct 30. Searches confirm no >5% drops have occurred between Oct 30 and Nov 9. This means the event must occur during the remaining ~35 trading days in 2025. Given the relatively high but decaying market estimate (from 11% with ~42 days left), a proportionate time decay brings the probability down slightly. With about a week and a half elapsed, the remaining probability of a severe 5% daily market crash before the end of the year is around 8%. S&P 500 daily drops exceeding 5% are extremely rare historical events, only occurring during severe volatility peaks (most recently April 2025). While baseline volatility remains slightly elevated, 8% captures the remaining risk accurately without overstating it, given the short time window left.
